title: Inside Google Antigravity 2.0: The complete developer guide | The Agent Factory
summary: Google Antigravity 2.0 is an agentic development platform that supports autonomous code generation and multi-agent workflows. It features a standalone desktop agent manager, CLI, SDK, and custom IDE that enable developers to scale output using AI agents with capabilities like parallel sub-agent orchestration, context compression through skills, and code review workflows.
writeup: This Google Cloud Tech video walks through Google Antigravity 2.0, an agentic software engineering platform that has unbundled from a single IDE into a standalone desktop agent manager, a CLI, an SDK, and a custom IDE. It demonstrates orchestrating parallel sub-agents for code generation, deployment, and testing, driven by voice prompts and slash commands. A key feature is context compression through reusable skills, which keeps agent context manageable across tasks. The shift from one IDE to a set of separate components signals how agentic development tooling is being restructured around multi-agent orchestration.
